title: Curaleaf Reports Second Quarter 2024 Results
text: Curaleaf Holdings reported Q2 2024 net revenue of $342.3 million, representing a modest 2% year-over-year increase. The company achieved an adjusted gross margin of 48%, up 253 basis points compared to the prior year period. Boris Jordan, Executive Chairman, stated: Second quarter revenue was $342 million, up 2% and adjusted gross margin of 48% was up 253 basis points compared to last year. He highlighted that June exited at 50% margin and adjusted EBITDA reached $73 million or 21.3%. CEO Matt Darin emphasized the company's evolution: We are at a pivotal time for our industry, on the cusp of federal reform, and the future is bright. He noted the international business achieved superb 78% year-over-year growth led by the UK, Germany. Net loss attributable to shareholders was $48.9 million ($0.06 per share), Adjusted EBITDA reached $73.0 million (21% margin), Operating cash flow from continuing operations was $30.2 million, Free cash flow from continuing operations was $6.0 million, and Cash position was $89.4 million. The company opened its 62nd dispensary in Florida and completed the Northern Green Canada acquisition. Notably, Curaleaf launched TheHempCompany.com, entering the hemp-derived THC market with products shipping to 25 states plus Washington, D.C.
